themes.name: Staffing Levels and Workload
themes.description: Comments related to the sufficiency of staffing, workload demands, overtime, coverage, burnout, and resource allocation to support effective patient care and manageable staff duties.

themes.name: Leadership and Management Practices
themes.description: Feedback about the behaviours, visibility, competence, and decision-making of all levels of management, including supportiveness, approachability, experience, and the ability to address or escalate issues.

themes.name: Career Development and Progression
themes.description: Recurring points on the availability, fairness, transparency, and accessibility of career advancement opportunities, training, support for further education or qualifications, and discussion of banding structures.

themes.name: Pay and Financial Reward
themes.description: Perspectives on pay, equity of pay across roles and bands, adequacy of salaries relative to role complexity and market, cost of living, and related benefits/rewards.

themes.name: Flexible, Hybrid and Remote Working
themes.description: Observations on the availability, fairness, consistency, and management of flexible, hybrid, remote, and family-friendly working arrangements, including issues with policies, implementation, and perceived inequity.

themes.name: Communication and Engagement
themes.description: Staff experiences with internal communication, information-sharing, involvement in decision-making, response to feedback, transparency, and the flow of information throughout the organisation.

themes.name: Organisational Change and Restructuring
themes.description: Responses focused on experiences of mergers, restructures, changes to roles, teams, processes, or job security, including the impact on morale and clarity.

themes.name: Organisational Culture and Values
themes.description: Impressions of the prevailing culture, including inclusivity, openness, respect, civility, trust values, 'tick box' mentality, perceived tokenism or disconnect between stated and lived values, and approaches to innovation.

themes.name: Bullying, Discrimination, and Equity
themes.description: Comments that raise concerns around bullying, harassment, favouritism, nepotism, lack of recognition, discrimination (including on the basis of race, disability, gender, or other protected characteristics), and fairness.

themes.name: Support for Health and Wellbeing
themes.description: Accounts regarding the organisation’s support (or lack thereof) for staff health and wellbeing, including mental health, disability, menopause, occupational health, return-to-work, and general wellbeing initiatives.

themes.name: Policies, Procedures and Bureaucracy
themes.description: Insights into the volume, clarity, and enforcement of policies and procedures (e.g. sickness, absence, performance, supervision, mandatory training, reporting systems), and their real or perceived impact on everyday work.

themes.name: Work Environment and Facilities
themes.description: Reflections on the physical and technological environment, such as office space, hot-desking, equipment, buildings, room availability, IT systems, parking, and other site-level or infrastructure issues.

themes.name: Team Dynamics and Peer Support
themes.description: Experiences relating to team atmosphere, morale, collaboration, informal support, supervision, workload distribution within teams, and relationships with immediate colleagues.

themes.name: Recognition and Feedback
themes.description: Focus on the presence, type, and adequacy of recognition, appreciation, and feedback mechanisms for staff contributions, efforts, or long service.

themes.name: Patient Care and Service Quality
themes.description: Staff perspectives on patient safety, standards of care, service models, patient complexity, resource constraints affecting care, and alignment of work or targets with service user needs.

themes.name: Recruitment, Onboarding and Retention
themes.description: Comments about the recruitment process, speed and quality of onboarding, staff retention, turnover, and the factors influencing decisions to join, remain, or leave the organisation.

themes.name: Sickness, Absence, and Attendance Management
themes.description: Discussion of sickness and attendance policies, including perceived leniency, strictness, process, impact on morale, and the handling of short and long-term absence.

themes.name: Equality, Diversity and Inclusion
themes.description: Remarks about the organisation’s approach to EDI, including practices, opportunities, diversity in leadership, support for neurodiverse or disabled staff, and the experience of international recruits.

themes.name: Administrative and Non-Clinical Roles
themes.description: Focus on experiences, value, recognition, career pathways, pay, and support for administrative and support staff relative to clinical roles.

themes.name: Systems and Digital Transformation
themes.description: Views on the implementation, adequacy, usability, and integration of digital systems, reporting tools, IT support, and the effects on workflow and staff experience.

themes.name: Trust Size and Organisational Structure
themes.description: Reflections on the effects of organisational scale, layers of management, centralisation, local autonomy, and the impact on communication, responsiveness, and identity.
